frameworks.name: voxel-wise encoding models
frameworks.one_line: deep-network visual features map to fMRI responses across cortex

frameworks.name: deep neural network feature extraction
frameworks.one_line: DNN supplies the visual feature basis used to predict brain responses

frameworks.name: random projection model of working memory
frameworks.one_line: random sensory-to-PFC projections account for amplified individual variability

frameworks.name: individual differences in visual perception
frameworks.one_line: behavioural idiosyncrasies framed as differing high-level neural codes

core_question: What is the lateral prefrontal cortex's role in coding visual features and why do individuals differ?
key_assumptions: DNN-derived features approximate the visual feature space the brain encodes
voxel-wise encoding can index high-level visual representations beyond visual cortex
sensory-to-PFC projections are approximately random across individuals
manipulations: across-subject comparison of encoding-model representational structures
image screening for highest predicted LPFC responses
key_findings: encoding models robustly predict responses in patches of lateral prefrontal cortex
LPFC shows larger individual differences in coding scheme than visual regions
screening identifies images that maximally drive each individual's LPFC
random-projection working-memory model reproduces the amplified LPFC variability
interpretations: LPFC contributes to visual processing beyond classical visual cortex
idiosyncratic random sensory-to-PFC projections may underlie individual perceptual differences
